documents.title: NSU Learning and Educational Center Generative AI
NSU Libraries Generative Artificial Intelligence guide

ai_strategy.summary: Nova Southeastern's public AI posture is concentrated in faculty-support, library, and research-practice resources rather than a highly centralized AI strategy. NSU provides faculty guidance through the Learning and Educational Center, library GenAI guides, Microsoft Copilot access for faculty and staff, and research/symposium activity around GenAI in education and qualitative research.

key_provisions: Use AI in accordance with academic integrity expectations and instructor or unit guidance
Do not enter restricted, confidential, regulated, or sensitive data into unapproved AI tools
Review AI outputs for accuracy, hallucination, bias, copyright, and attribution issues
Disclose or cite AI use when required by course, research, publisher, or unit rules
Keep human judgment and accountability in consequential academic, research, and administrative decisions
ai_policy.summary: Nova Southeastern University's AI policy posture is best characterized as permissive: public sources show explicit expectations for responsible use, privacy, disclosure, academic integrity, and human review, while leaving some implementation decisions to units or instructors.

initiatives.description: NSU-affiliated scholarship studies medical educators' perceptions of generative AI in medical education, framing GenAI as a disruptive force for faculty and learners.

initiatives.title: NSU AI site and AI-integrated programs
initiatives.description: NSU’s AI site presents a university-wide AI vision and lists AI-integrated programs including an undergraduate AI minor and M.S. in Artificial Intelligence.
initiatives.significance: This strengthens curriculum evidence and moves NSU beyond library guidance.

initiatives.title: Learning design guidance for AI-resilient assessment
initiatives.description: NSU’s Learning and Educational Center guidance recommends restructuring courses, using multi-stage projects, discussing AI expectations, and using Microsoft Copilot for protected student-work analysis.
initiatives.significance: This closes the assessment-redesign gap with explicit faculty-facing learning-design guidance.

regulatory_context.summary: Nova Southeastern University operates in Florida; public AI guidance should be read alongside applicable state, federal, and institutional privacy, security, accessibility, and academic-integrity requirements.

narrative_summary: Nova Southeastern University (Fort Lauderdale, FL) is an R1 institution whose public AI posture shows activity across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tooling, workforce readiness, and access. Nova Southeastern's public AI posture is concentrated in faculty-support, library, and research-practice resources rather than a highly centralized AI strategy. NSU provides faculty guidance through the Learning and Educational Center, library GenAI guides, Microsoft Copilot access for faculty and staff, and research/symposium activity around GenAI in education and qualitative research. The most concrete public initiatives captured here are Learning and Educational Center GenAI guidance; Microsoft Copilot access; NSU Libraries Generative AI guide; Integrating AI and GenAI across the curriculum scholarship; Generative AI in medical education research. Across the profile, the strongest evidence comes from official university AI hubs, policy pages, teaching resources, research-center pages, and program pages rather than secondary reporting. The institution_type is set to r1_research from the canonical Carnegie R1 list. Known gaps are intentionally explicit: leadership names, user counts, detailed budgets, and compute counts are only included when they surfaced in public sources. Overall, the profile supports at least five AI initiatives and evidences strategy/leadership, governance, teaching/curriculum, research infrastructure, student services or enterprise tooling, workforce/community, partnerships, and equity dimensions where public sources permit.

gaps_acknowledged: No centralized NSU AI strategy document, chief AI officer, or AI investment amount was found.
Evidence is strongest for teaching guidance and library resources, weaker for enterprise-scale AI transformation.
Enrollment was left null rather than relying on non-source local data.
NSU now has clear Copilot and assessment-design evidence, but public sources still did not disclose Copilot user counts, contract values, or dedicated AI/HPC GPU capacity.
Worker 3 did not find public subgroup-level outcome measures for NSU AI literacy or AI-integrated programs.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: governance led.
